title: Fix SELinux labeling in libpod to correctly reserve labels
body:
Also update some missing fields libpod.conf obtions in man pages.

Fix sort order of security options and add a note about disabling
labeling.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Fixes https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1401

@TomSweeneyRedHat Existing tests will make sure labeling is still on.
We currently do not have a decent way to change the tests to use a non system libpod.conf.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 2e6243b) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1464) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Seeing a lot of errors building buildah stuff
@mheon Check it out now. I got it to work by passing down "z" and "Z" options.  And removing them in generateSpec
Bunch of small stuff, but the direction seems solid to me now - few comments and minor fixes and we're good to merge
I'm still not sure what's going on with the tests and buildah, but it seems to be affecting multiple PRs
@mheon I merged in a patch that should fix the buildah issues,  Will see if this tests correctly.

@rhatdan: The following test **failed**, say `/retest` to rerun them all:

Test name | Commit | Details | Rerun command
--- | --- | --- | ---

Ok this is now passing tests.

@mheon @TomSweeneyRedHat @umohnani8 @giuseppe @baude PTAL
Looks like a test failure still @rhatdan but I'm not sure what's popping it.
LGTM otherwise
Labelling and Labeling are both valid according to https://www.grammar.com/labelled_vs._labeled
@rhatdan pulls the British spelling card again and wins!  It was red lining for me in gmail and dictionary.com listed only one l
@mheon @baude @giuseppe @umohnani8 PTAL
LGTM. I'm concerned about what this does to our perf, but we can look into that later.
@rh-atomic-bot r=mheon
